signals.idea: Multi-select feature parity: the bulk properties panel should expose all the same settings as single-clip editing (effects, position/crop, text, transitions, entrance/exit/continuous animations, volume slider, from/to timing, etc.), not just opacity/speed/mute. Also ensure all single-clip operations work with multi-selection where it makes sense: arrow key track movement, context menu operations (copy, split-at-playhead for each), and live visual feedback during multi-drag. The BulkClipPanel in PropertiesPanel.tsx currently only shows 3 fields — it needs to match the full ClipPanel feature set with mixed-value handling.

unresolved_flags.concern: Bulk editing: nested `ClipMeta` patches are shallow-merged, so the plan's assumption that `handleUpdateClips` already supports bulk `text`/`entrance`/`exit`/`continuous` updates is incorrect.
Bulk panel tabs: the proposed visibility rules diverge from the actual single-clip behavior, so the bulk panel would lose parity for common selections.

Properties parity: the plan equates parity with mirroring `ClipPanel`, but the repository's single-clip properties UI does not currently expose crop or transition controls, even though the idea explicitly calls those out.
Context menu: the plan drops requested multi-copy support even though the idea explicitly includes copy and the repository has no existing clip copy/paste workflow to fall back on.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 1. Weighted score trajectory: 7.0. Plan deltas: n/a. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 0. Open significant flags: 4.

warnings: Scope creep detected: the plan appears to be expanding beyond the original idea or recorded user notes.

items: BulkClipPanel shows tabbed UI matching ClipPanel's structure (Effects, Timing, Position, Audio, Text tabs) when 2+ clips are selected
All applicable ClipMeta fields are editable in bulk: entrance/exit/continuous effects, speed, from/to, x/y/width/height, opacity, volume, text fontSize/color
Mixed values display 'Mixed' placeholder; editing a field applies the new value to all selected clips
Tab visibility is determined by the union of selected clip types (e.g., Audio tab only if any clip is on an audio track)
Arrow up/down keys move ALL selected clips to adjacent track of matching kind
Context menu shows 'Split at Playhead' for multi-select (splits each clip that intersects playhead)
TypeScript compiles without errors after all changes
Undo correctly reverts bulk edits for all affected clips

unresolved_flags.concern: Bulk editing: nested `ClipMeta` patches are shallow-merged, so the plan's assumption that `handleUpdateClips` already supports bulk `text`/`entrance`/`exit`/`continuous` updates is incorrect.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useClipEditing.ts:89-105` applies the exact same patch object to every selected clip, and `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineSave.ts:220-239` only does a top-level spread merge. In the existing single-clip UI, nested fields are preserved manually per clip (`src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/ClipPanel.tsx:126-183` and `:275-295`). A bulk patch like `{ text: { fontSize: 48 } }` would overwrite each clip's existing text content/color/alignment.

unresolved_flags.concern: Bulk panel tabs: the proposed visibility rules diverge from the actual single-clip behavior, so the bulk panel would lose parity for common selections.
unresolved_flags.evidence: The plan says to show Audio only when any selected clip is on an audio track, but `getVisibleClipTabs` in `src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/ClipPanel.tsx:37-49` shows the Audio tab for non-text clips on visual tracks as well. A multi-selection of ordinary video clips on visual tracks would incorrectly lose bulk volume controls under the proposed rule.

unresolved_flags.concern: Properties parity: the plan equates parity with mirroring `ClipPanel`, but the repository's single-clip properties UI does not currently expose crop or transition controls, even though the idea explicitly calls those out.
unresolved_flags.evidence: `src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/ClipPanel.tsx:119-305` only renders effects, timing, position, audio, and text controls. Crop reset/fill lives in the overlay editor instead (`src/tools/video-editor/components/PreviewPanel/OverlayEditor.tsx:700-741`), and `rg -n "transition" src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor src/tools/video-editor/hooks` found no transition-editing UI. Mirroring `ClipPanel` will still miss requested crop/transition parity.

unresolved_flags.concern: Context menu: the plan drops requested multi-copy support even though the idea explicitly includes copy and the repository has no existing clip copy/paste workflow to fall back on.
unresolved_flags.evidence: The idea calls out context-menu copy for multi-selection, but the plan's assumptions mark copy/paste out of scope. In the current implementation, `src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx:143-195` only exposes split, mute/unmute, and delete, and `rg -n "copy|paste|clipboard" src/tools/video-editor` found no clip-level copy/paste flow to preserve parity with.
